fusion_hci.name: IBM Fusion HCI
fusion_hci.description: Engineered, turnkey hyper-converged platform that fuses bare-metal Red Hat OpenShift, integrated Fusion data-service operators, NVMe storage, and optional GPU acceleration. Runs containers, virtual machines and generative-AI workloads on-premises.
key_features: NVMe storage with optional GPU acceleration

use_cases.name: AI Inference / Generative-AI (on-prem)
use_cases.description: Run AI workloads on-premises with GPU acceleration
use_cases.benefit: Meets data-sovereignty and latency requirements while leveraging GPU-accelerated nodes

use_cases.name: OpenShift Resiliency
use_cases.description: Add HA, policy-driven backup and DR to container platforms
use_cases.benefit: Differentiates IBM from pure-cloud OpenShift offerings

use_cases.name: VM Consolidation
use_cases.description: Run legacy VM workloads alongside modern containers
use_cases.benefit: Allows coexistence of legacy and modern workloads on a single stack

use_cases.name: Data-Lakehouse / Asset-Life-Cycle Pipelines
use_cases.description: Scalable storage for data pipelines and analytics
use_cases.benefit: Accelerates pipeline execution and reduces OPEX with NVMe storage

use_cases.name: Hybrid-Cloud Burst Capacity
use_cases.description: Self-service cluster provisioning for dynamic workloads
use_cases.benefit: Spin up resources in days-to-weeks, avoiding over-provisioning

competitive_advantages: Single-vendor, integrated stack eliminates complexity of multiple vendors
GitOps-driven UI provides unified, code-centric management
Scalable, data-sovereign architecture with watsonx integration
80-90% TCO savings compared to traditional infrastructure
Dramatically faster time-to-value for AI pipelines and data-lakehouse use cases
Built-in Red Hat AI and IBM Cloud Paks integration
benefits: Lower total cost of ownership (80-90% savings)

technical_specs.storage: NVMe storage with integrated data-service operators
technical_specs.acceleration: Optional GPU acceleration for AI workloads

common_objections.response: Fusion HCI allows you to run both VMs and containers on the same platform, providing a migration path while maintaining existing investments
For data-sovereign workloads and AI inference, Fusion HCI delivers 80-90% TCO savings compared to cloud alternatives while meeting compliance requirements
Fusion HCI's GitOps-driven UI and full-stack automation dramatically simplifies day-1 and day-2 operations compared to DIY OpenShift deployments

common_objections.objection: We're not ready for AI workloads
common_objections.response: Fusion HCI is infrastructure-ready for when you are, while delivering immediate benefits for container and VM workloads today

fusion_hci.pricing_model: Contact IBM for custom pricing based on configuration and scale

powervs.name: IBM Power Virtual Server
powervs.description: IBM's cloud-based Power virtual server offering for AIX, IBM i, and Linux workloads. Provides enterprise-grade performance and reliability in the cloud.
key_features: Support for AIX, IBM i, and Linux
Same-silicon continuity with on-premises Power systems

use_cases.name: Hybrid-Cloud Extension
use_cases.description: Extend on-premises Power workloads to the cloud
use_cases.benefit: Maintain application compatibility while gaining cloud flexibility

use_cases.name: SAP Migration
use_cases.description: Migrate SAP workloads to cloud-based Power infrastructure
use_cases.benefit: Reduce infrastructure costs while maintaining performance

use_cases.name: AI/ML Workloads
use_cases.description: Run AI and machine learning workloads on Power architecture
use_cases.benefit: Leverage Power's performance for data-intensive AI applications

use_cases.name: Disaster Recovery
use_cases.description: Cloud-based DR for on-premises Power systems
use_cases.benefit: Cost-effective business continuity without duplicate hardware

competitive_advantages: Same-silicon continuity with on-premises Power systems
Native support for AIX and IBM i

technical_specs.integration: Native watsonx and IBM Cloud integration

common_objections.objection: Cloud is not secure enough for our workloads
common_objections.response: PowerVS provides enterprise-grade security with 99.999% availability and integrated compliance features
Same-silicon continuity ensures application compatibility, and IBM provides migration services and tools

flashsystem.name: IBM FlashSystem
flashsystem.description: IBM's enterprise-class all-flash storage platform delivering high-performance, low-latency storage for mission-critical workloads.

use_cases.name: High-Performance Databases
use_cases.description: Accelerate database workloads with NVMe storage
use_cases.benefit: Dramatically reduce query times and improve application performance

use_cases.name: Analytics & AI
use_cases.description: Fast storage for data analytics and AI training
use_cases.benefit: Reduce time-to-insight and accelerate AI model training

use_cases.name: Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI)
use_cases.benefit: Deliver consistent user experience with low latency

use_cases.name: Backup & Disaster Recovery
use_cases.description: Fast, reliable backup and recovery infrastructure
use_cases.benefit: Reduce backup windows and recovery time objectives

competitive_advantages: AI-driven predictive analytics in support program

common_objections.response: FlashSystem's data reduction and lower operational costs deliver better TCO than traditional storage

common_objections.objection: We don't need that level of performance
common_objections.response: FlashSystem's performance enables new use cases and future-proofs your infrastructure as workloads grow

flashsystem.pricing_model: Contact IBM for custom pricing based on capacity and features
